note: Objective answer key. All numbers computed from the SHIPPED sources/palettes.json by references/reference_analysis.py using Machado-2009 colorblind simulation, CIE76 deltaE on min pairwise distance, and WCAG relative luminance for grayscale separation. Offline + frozen -> deterministic, never stale. Grade on ORDINAL outcomes; absolute numbers vary by method.

hard_constraints.HC1_red_green_separation: minimum pairwise CIE76 deltaE across the 5 colors must be >= 15 under BOTH deuteranopia and protanopia simulation (colors stay distinct for red-green colorblind viewers)
hard_constraints.HC2_grayscale_separation: minimum pairwise grayscale luminance separation must be >= 5 (on a 0..100 scale) so the palette still works when printed in black and white

soft_constraints.SC1_max_worst_case_rg: prefer larger worst-case (min of deut/prot) pairwise deltaE

per_candidate_results.trap: RAINBOW/JET: looks colorful and even has decent color separation, but is perceptually non-uniform and collapses in grayscale (1.6). The classic 'never use rainbow for science' trap.
THE FAMOUS 'COLORBLIND-SAFE' PALETTE: passes the red-green test (17.0), so a knowledgeable picker grabs it — but it FAILS the grayscale/print requirement (1.1). Expert-knowledge trap.
pastels: low saturation, poor separation everywhere

tiebreak.reason: Among the two survivors, viridis wins BOTH soft constraints (worst-case red-green 19.4 > 16.6 AND grayscale separation 6.9 > 6.3).

gold.acceptable_alternative: Paired (the only other hard-pass survivor) with an explicit correct tradeoff -> up to ~0.85

key_facts.jet_is_a_trap: must NOT be selected; fails grayscale despite colorful appearance
key_facts.okabe_ito_is_a_trap: must NOT be selected; the famous CB-safe palette fails the grayscale/print requirement here

scoring_anchors.tolerance_note: Grade ordinal outcomes: jet fails grayscale, okabe_ito fails grayscale (not red-green), the Set/Accent/Pastel ColorBrewer qualitative schemes fail red-green, survivors are viridis+Paired, viridis is optimal. Exact deltaE/luminance numbers may differ by method.
